title: for loop
instruction:
Classic indexed for loop:

```csharp
for (init; condition; update)
```

Run this.
successMessage: for loops have three parts: initialiser (int i=1), condition (i<=5), update (i++). All are optional.

title: Count down
instruction:
Loops can count backwards.

Run this.
successMessage: i-- decrements. The loop continues as long as i >= 1.

title: while loop
instruction:
`while` loops while a condition is true.

Run this.
successMessage: Doubling from 1: 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128. The first power of 2 greater than 100 is 128.

title: do-while loop
instruction:
`do-while` always runs at least once — the condition is checked after.

Run this.
successMessage: do-while is for situations where you need at least one execution — like 'show menu, then ask again if invalid input'.

title: foreach loop
instruction:
`foreach` iterates over any collection.

Run this.
successMessage: foreach is the cleanest way to iterate — no index arithmetic, no off-by-one risk. Use it for collections whenever you do not need the index.

title: break and continue
instruction:
`break` exits the loop. `continue` skips to the next iteration.

Run this.
successMessage: Multiples of 3 are skipped (continue). The loop stops after 7 (break). 3, 6, and 8+ are not printed.

title: Nested loops
instruction:
Loops can be nested. Use descriptive variable names.

Run this.
successMessage: A 3x3 multiplication table. Inner loop runs completely for each outer loop iteration.

title: Loop over indices with LINQ range
instruction:
Use `Enumerable.Range` for index-based loops without a traditional for.

Run this.
successMessage: Enumerable.Range(1,5) generates 1,2,3,4,5. Select transforms each. This is the LINQ way of a for loop — useful in method chains.
